content: Active agentic retrieval systems can be redesigned to treat working memory capacity limits not as a flaw to fix, but as a built-in query pruning mechanism that automatically filters out low-priority subqueries during complex query decomposition. This naturally reduces search overload and aligns the retrieval pipeline with the agent's bounded cognitive budget.
source_topics: Active Agentic Retrieval with Search Agents
agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: Active Agentic Retrieval with Search Agents
